Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Fatimah's communications with Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest about the Capitol Hill apartment booking. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Carmen UrbanNest or Capitol Hill apartment or lease (or similar). The agent discovers Carmen's original housing options email listing three Seattle apartments including Option A at 412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B, Capitol Hill at $2,100 per month. The agent also finds Fatimah's confirmation reply selecting Option A. These emails reference Noah Fitzgerald and GreenStack Energy as the tenant and employer.
Search emails for the relocation confirmation Fatimah sent to the client to trace what details were delivered. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like relocation package confirmed or move to Seattle or 412 Boylston (or similar). The agent discovers Fatimah's confirmation email sent to Noah (at n.fitzpatrick@axiomprecision.com) containing: apartment at 412 Boylston Ave E Apt 3B Capitol Hill, flight ACT-77492 on Alaska Airlines AS 422 departing April 14, and UrbanNest contact Priya Shenoy. This confirmation was sent to Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision Manufacturing but the details (apartment, flight) belong to Noah Fitzgerald's GreenStack Energy relocation. This is the root cause of the mix-up: Fatimah pulled the wrong file.
Search emails for all correspondence mentioning Noah Fitzpatrick and Axiom Precision to understand the Axiom relocation thread. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Noah Fitzpatrick or Axiom Precision or Carla Mendenhall (or similar). The agent discovers: Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR Director) responded to Emeka's cold outreach requesting a relocation for Noah Fitzpatrick from Philadelphia to Seattle, Emeka forwarded the intake to Fatimah, Fatimah sent Noah Fitzpatrick an intake questionnaire, and Noah Fitzpatrick later forwarded the (wrong) confirmation to Carla who praised the fast turnaround and asked for a services agreement. Carla Mendenhall's email address (c.mendenhall@axiomprecision.com) is obtainable from this thread.
OE 4: Search emails for all correspondence mentioning Noah Fitzgerald and GreenStack Energy to understand the parallel relocation thread. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like Noah Fitzgerald or GreenStack Seattle or Derek Hollis (or similar). The agent discovers Noah Fitzgerald's GreenStack relocation from Philadelphia to Seattle with move-in target of April 28-29. Derek Hollis at GreenStack Energy is the People Operations Manager. The agent should notice that BOTH Noahs are relocating from Philadelphia to Seattle in the same timeframe, creating the confusion.
Search Slack for the operations channel discussion where Fatimah asked about the two Noah names. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald or Axiom Noah (or similar). The agent discovers Fatimah's Slack message asking Emeka whether the Axiom employee is Fitzpatrick or Fitzgerald, and Emeka confirming Fitzpatrick is Axiom and Fitzgerald is GreenStack. Despite this explicit clarification, Fatimah still pulled the wrong file when setting up the confirmation.
Search Airtable for Noah Fitzgerald's relocation record to check its current status. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Noah Fitzgerald. Record recReloc00000006 shows: Company GreenStack Energy, Status Completed, Origin Philadelphia PA, Destination Seattle WA, Move Start Date April 28, Move End Date April 29, Assigned Coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi. The Status is incorrectly marked Completed because the move-in is April 28 which is 2 days from the universe date of April 26 and has not yet occurred. The record was never updated since creation on April 2.
Search Airtable for Noah Fitzpatrick's relocation record. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Name, value Noah Fitzpatrick. No record exists. Despite the relocation being coordinated and confirmed, a relocation record for Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision was never created in Airtable.
Search Airtable Client Accounts for Axiom Precision Manufacturing to check the account status. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Client Accounts, field_name Client, value Axiom. Record recAcct000000013 shows: Status Cold Outreach, Total Relocations 0, Account Manager Emeka Diallo. The account was never updated from Cold Outreach to Active despite the Fitzpatrick relocation being fully coordinated and confirmed.
Search CRM for Axiom Precision Manufacturing company record to check its current type and status. Use crm_search_companies with name Axiom. The CRM company record (company_axiom) shows Type Prospect. Despite the Fitzpatrick relocation being fully coordinated and Emeka's self-memo claiming the deal was updated to CLOSED WON, the company type was never changed from Prospect.
Search CRM for any Axiom Precision Manufacturing deals to verify whether Emeka's claimed deal actually exists. Use crm_search_deals with company_id company_axiom (or dealname Axiom). No deal record is found for Axiom in CRM. Emeka's email to Fatimah mentions he created an Axiom CRM deal at Prospect status with estimated value $12,500, and his self-memo claims he updated it to CLOSED WON on 2026-02-03, but neither the deal creation nor the status update is reflected in the actual CRM data.
Search Linear for tickets related to Noah Fitzgerald's GreenStack relocation. Use linear_list_issues with query containing keywords like Noah Fitzgerald or GreenStack Seattle or Fitzgerald apartment (or similar). The agent discovers: apartment search ticket number 316 (state in_progress, due April 20 which is 6 days overdue), flight booking ticket number 317 (state todo, due April 21 which is 5 days overdue), and movers coordination ticket number 318 (state todo, due April 22 which is 4 days overdue). These tickets show the GreenStack relocation has not been fully completed in the tracking system despite the Airtable record showing Completed.
Search Linear for any tickets related to Noah Fitzpatrick or Axiom Precision. Use linear_list_issues with query containing keywords like Fitzpatrick or Axiom (or similar). Fatimah's email to Emeka references updating the Axiom/Fitzpatrick Linear ticket, but the agent should verify whether this ticket actually exists in Linear or if it is another manifestation of the cross-contamination where Fitzgerald's ticket was used.
Look up contact details for Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest, Emeka Diallo, and Derek Hollis at GreenStack. Use contacts_search_contacts with query Carmen Reyes, then Emeka Diallo, then Derek Hollis. Carmen Reyes is carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com. Emeka Diallo is emeka.diallo@moveops.com. Derek Hollis is derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com. Note: Carla Mendenhall at Axiom does not have a record in the contacts database — her email (c.mendenhall@axiomprecision.com) is obtained from the email discovery in OE 3 (Emeka's intake email to Fatimah).
Update Noah Fitzgerald's Airtable relocation record to correct the status from Completed to In Progress. Use airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblRelocations01, records including recReloc00000006 with Status changed to In Progress and Special Requirements updated to note the lease name mix-up and that the apartment was given to the wrong Noah.
Create a new Airtable relocation record for Noah Fitzpatrick at Axiom Precision Manufacturing since no record exists. Use airtable_create_record with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, fields including Name Noah Fitzpatrick, Company Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Status In Progress, Origin Philadelphia PA, Destination Seattle WA, Assigned Coordinator Fatimah Al-Rashidi.
Update the Axiom Precision Manufacturing Client Accounts record to reflect the active relocation. Use airtable_update_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_id tblClientAccts01, records including recAcct000000013 with Status changed from Cold Outreach to Active and Total Relocations changed from 0 to 1.
Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest with the corrected lease details. Use send_email with sender fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, recipients including carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com. Content should explain that the lease paperwork needs to be updated with the correct tenant details, provide the corrected information (Noah Fitzpatrick, Axiom Precision Manufacturing instead of Noah Fitzgerald, GreenStack Energy), and ask Carmen to reissue the lease with the correct information.
Send an email to Emeka Diallo notifying him about the mix-up since he is the account manager for both Axiom and GreenStack. Use send_email with sender fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, recipients including emeka.diallo@moveops.com. Content should explain the two-Noah cross-contamination, what was sent incorrectly, what has been fixed, and flag that Noah Fitzgerald at GreenStack may not have a valid apartment booking for his April 28 move-in since his details were given to the Axiom employee. Emeka needs to assess whether Carla Mendenhall at Axiom and Derek Hollis at GreenStack need to be contacted.
Post a message in the operations Slack channel flagging the mix-up for team visibility. Use conversations_add_message with channel_id C006, payload describing the two-Noah name confusion, the affected records across systems, what has been corrected, and what still needs urgent attention (specifically that Noah Fitzgerald's April 28 move-in may be at risk because his apartment was assigned to the wrong person).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✓Run #3The email to Carmen explicitly states 'Tenant Name: Noah Fitzpatrick' and 'Employer: Axiom Precision Manufacturing', replacing the incorrect 'Noah Fitzgerald / GreenStack Energy'.

✓Run #5The email to Carmen explicitly states that 412 Boylston Ave E, Apt 3B should remain under 'Noah Fitzpatrick / Axiom Precision Manufacturing' and asks Carmen to disregard the Fitzgerald booking. It correctly identifies Fitzpatrick as the tenant and Axiom Precision Manufacturing as the employer.

22 When searching Airtable for relocation records, the model must search the Relocations table (or a table name related to relocations or moves, or similar) rather than the Stipend Transactions or Client Accounts table.
query construction
